page: Microsoft Azure E128s_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Esv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a substantial cache hierarchy, consisting of 48 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 480 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and integer operations. The instance is equipped with 1024.0 GB of system memory, providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10%. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and PassMark memory scores landing in the middle 50%. The instance demonstrates high throughput in data processing tasks, achieving top-tier results in gzip compression (36.22 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (704.78 GB/sec multi-threaded). Database and memory-bound operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by 189.85 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and a top-tier static web serving throughput of 165.05 GB/sec. For artificial intelligence workloads, the hardware achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds of 45.31 tokens per second on a large 70B LLM, though text generation speeds remain average at 4.79 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E128s_v7 offers high resource density, combining massive memory capacity with a high-vCPU Intel Xeon processor. This makes it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that require substantial parallel processing power without the need for bundled local storage. The lack of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring external premium storage attachment for persistent data. The instance is suited for large-scale database deployments, in-memory caching systems like Redis, high-throughput web serving, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure E128s_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance from the Esv7 family, powered by a 128-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 1024.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside a 480 MB L3 cache and support for AVX-512 and AMX instructions. While it lacks bundled local storage and public IPv4 addresses, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional bzip3 decompression speeds, and high-throughput Redis operations reaching 189.85 million operations per second. Conversely, single-core CPU and memory latency benchmarks align with average tiers. The instance is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, and parallel data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E128s_v7 is a memory-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E128s_v7 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core CPU and Redis performance.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized virtual server featuring 128 vCPUs and 1 TB of RAM for demanding database and parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs and 1024 GB of RAM providing a 1:8 resource ratio.
Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 480 MB L3 cache and AMX/AVX-512 support.
Top-tier multi-core performance and 189.85 million Redis operations per second.
No bundled local storage, requiring external Premium Storage configuration.
Suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and high-throughput web serving.
